Line Charts

VSCode

The examples below all use the following semantic model.

source: flights is duckdb.table('../data/flights.parquet') extend {
  join_one: carriers is duckdb.table('../data/carriers.parquet') 
    on carrier = carriers.code
  measure: 
    flight_count is count()
    aircraft_count is count(tail_num)
}

Line charts take two or three parameters.

  • First parameter - X-axis is time field or numeric expression

  • Second parameter - Y-axis is a numeric expression

  • Third (optional) Pivot is dimensional field (numeric or string)

Single line line_chart 

# line_chart
run: flights -> {
  group_by: departure_month is dep_time.month
  aggregate: flight_count
}

]
SELECT 
   DATE_TRUNC('month', base."dep_time") as "departure_month",
   COUNT(1) as "flight_count"
FROM '../data/flights.parquet' as base
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 desc NULLS LAST

Multi line line_chart

The third parameter in a line chart adds a line for each dimension found.

# line_chart
run: flights -> {
  group_by: departure_month is dep_time.month
  aggregate: flight_count
  group_by: carriers.nickname
}
QUERY RESULTS 
[
  {
    "departure_month": "2005-12-01T00:00:00.000Z",

SELECT 
   DATE_TRUNC('month', base."dep_time") as "departure_month",
   COUNT(1) as "flight_count",
   carriers_0."nickname" as "nickname"
FROM '../data/flights.parquet' as base
 LEFT JOIN '../data/carriers.parquet' AS carriers_0
  ON base."carrier"=carriers_0."code"
GROUP BY 1,3
ORDER BY 1 desc NULLS LAST

Line Charts nested in tables

When line charts are nested in tables the size is reduced. Nested line charts increase the density of information provided by the result.

run: flights -> {
  group_by: destination
  aggregate: flight_count
  # line_chart
  nest: by_month is  {
    group_by: departure_month is dep_time.month
    aggregate: flight_count
    group_by: carriers.nickname
  }
  limit: 5
}
QUERY RESULTS
destinationflight_​countby_​month

]
WITH __stage0 AS (
  SELECT
    group_set,
    base."destination" as "destination__0",
    CASE WHEN group_set=0 THEN
      COUNT(1)
      END as "flight_count__0",
    CASE WHEN group_set=1 THEN
      DATE_TRUNC('month', base."dep_time")
      END as "departure_month__1",
    CASE WHEN group_set=1 THEN
      carriers_0."nickname"
      END as "nickname__1",
    CASE WHEN group_set=1 THEN
      COUNT(1)
      END as "flight_count__1"
  FROM '../data/flights.parquet' as base
   LEFT JOIN '../data/carriers.parquet' AS carriers_0
    ON base."carrier"=carriers_0."code"
  CROSS JOIN (SELECT UNNEST(GENERATE_SERIES(0,1,1)) as group_set  ) as group_set
  GROUP BY 1,2,4,5
)
SELECT
  "destination__0" as "destination",
  MAX(CASE WHEN group_set=0 THEN "flight_count__0" END) as "flight_count",
  COALESCE(LIST({
    "departure_month": "departure_month__1", 
    "flight_count": "flight_count__1", 
    "nickname": "nickname__1"}  ORDER BY  "departure_month__1" desc NULLS LAST) FILTER (WHERE group_set=1),[]) as "by_month"
FROM __stage0
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 desc NULLS LAST
LIMIT 5
